title: rubus × subreticulatus
intro: Rubus × subreticulatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry or Himalayan bramble, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and prevent infection. The health benefits of Rubus × subreticulatus include its ability to reduce blood sugar levels, lower cholesterol, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a potential natural remedy for various health conditions. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, juice, or dried fruit, which can be consumed fresh or used in herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
